jokes.text: Kent Brockman, who's contractually permitted to replace himself with a cardboard cutout. The real Kent is in a rehab clinic. We all wish him the best... Again.
jokes.setup: Standard holiday greeting from news anchor
jokes.punchline: Revealing the anchor is fake and the real one is repeatedly in rehab
jokes.explanation: Sharp media satire with perfect escalation - 'Again' is the killer detail that sells the recurring nature

jokes.text: See ya in spring, toes.
jokes.setup: Character bundling up for winter
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing and saying goodbye to their toes
jokes.explanation: Simple but charming character moment with great economy

jokes.text: For legal purposes, sick kids may include hookers with a cold.
jokes.setup: Krusty promising to be nice to sick kids for charity
jokes.punchline: Legal loophole that completely undermines the charitable intent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty - cynical, inappropriate, but delivered with his characteristic sleazy charm. The 'legal purposes' phrasing makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Apparently working in Hawaii was a pleasure.
jokes.setup: Describing Magnum PI DVD commentary
jokes.punchline: Understated observation about obvious tropical paradise job perk
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor, functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: Oh, Carl, you remembered I like TV.
jokes.setup: Homer receiving a thoughtful gift
jokes.punchline: Reducing all of television to a simple preference he has
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer - his childlike simplicity about his own interests is perfectly in character

jokes.text: May the spirit of Retsyn be with you all year long. God bless God. Amen.
jokes.setup: Homer giving a blessing with his cheap gift
jokes.punchline: Treating Certs breath mint ingredient as religious and blessing God himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd religious parody - 'God bless God' is particularly inspired nonsense

jokes.text: Homer, you're the most selfish man I know. Oh, come on. Mister Burns is way more selfish.
jokes.setup: Lenny calling Homer selfish
jokes.punchline: Homer's defense is comparing himself to a cartoonishly evil boss
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's warped perspective on moral standards

jokes.text: That evil old bone bag smelling of death, nose like a vulture, followed everywhere by that kiss-ass Smithers.
jokes.setup: Homer describing Mr. Burns to justify his behavior
jokes.punchline: Increasingly vivid and harsh description building to Smithers
jokes.explanation: Good escalating insult sequence with vivid imagery

jokes.text: Yes. That describes Kathy in Personnel to a tee.
jokes.setup: Homer's harsh description of Burns
jokes.punchline: Burns agreeing but applying it to a random employee instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - completely oblivious to Homer talking about him, redirects to petty office grievance

jokes.text: It's time for your Christmas 'bon-i.' Everyone gets a five-dollar cafeteria voucher.
jokes.setup: Burns announcing Christmas bonuses
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'bonus' and giving pathetically small cafeteria credit
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns - out of touch pronunciation and miserly gift perfectly in character

jokes.text: Joe DiMaggio? Yes. It seems they've started letting ethnics into the big leagues.
jokes.setup: Burns looking at a baseball card
jokes.punchline: Casual racist comment about DiMaggio as if integration just happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character work - his ancient perspective makes 1940s seem recent

jokes.text: I'm sorry. But I'm afraid your card is only worth everything I've got! Everything! Take it!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy appraising seemingly worthless old card
jokes.punchline: Dramatic reversal - it's incredibly valuable and he loses all composure
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect structure - the pause before 'everything' sells it

jokes.text: Oh, no! I've smudged it with nacho fingers. I must deftly lick it off. Deftly...
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy damaging the valuable card
jokes.punchline: His pretentious word choice while doing something gross and clumsy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy - pseudo-intellectual language for crude action, 'deftly' repetition is great

jokes.text: Oh, pruny night / The stars are sweetly wrinkled
jokes.setup: Christmas special featuring California Prunes
jokes.punchline: Prune-themed version of 'O Holy Night' with wrinkled stars
jokes.explanation: Clever parody with good wordplay - 'sweetly wrinkled' is inspired

jokes.text: This is offensive to Christians and prunes.
jokes.setup: Watching the prune Christmas special
jokes.punchline: Treating prunes as an equal group that could be offended
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge line - her earnest concern extended to fruit is absurd but in character

jokes.text: Let's shop till we droop. I think that's 'drop.' That's a very violent image, Lisa.
jokes.setup: Homer misquoting shopping phrase, Lisa correcting
jokes.punchline: Marge finding the correct version more disturbing than Homer's version
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic with Marge's characteristic overthinking

jokes.text: then we'll spend the rest of the cash on a Christmas tree so large, its absence from the forest will cause mudslides and flooding!
jokes.setup: Homer planning to buy a big Christmas tree
jokes.punchline: Escalating to environmental disaster scale
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer excess taken to absurd environmental consequences

jokes.text: Awesome! I'm blasting all the state capitals! Oh, take that, Salem, Oregon! Wait a minute. This game is educational! That'll teach you to teach me!
jokes.setup: Bart enjoying a video game
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's learning and getting angry about being tricked into education
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - his anti-learning stance is so strong he gets mad at accidentally being educated

jokes.text: Oh, God! It's so unnecessary!
jokes.setup: Homer seeing an expensive gadget
jokes.punchline: Recognizing it's unnecessary but wanting it anyway
jokes.explanation: Good Homer self-awareness that doesn't stop his behavior

jokes.text: I'm upside-down so much!
jokes.setup: Salesman mentioning pen works upside-down
jokes.punchline: Homer acting like being upside-down is a regular occurrence for him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - treating upside-down as a lifestyle he frequently experiences

jokes.text: Today is the birthday of comedienne Margaret Cho. That's the birthday I'm always forgetting! I must have it!
jokes.setup: Talking astrolabe giving random trivia
jokes.punchline: Homer pretending this obscure fact is something he desperately needed
jokes.explanation: Good Homer rationalization - inventing a need to justify wanting something

jokes.text: Now, everyone just relax, lock your doors, don't make eye contact with anyone, and listen to the radio.
jokes.setup: Homer telling family to relax in bad neighborhood
jokes.punchline: His 'relaxing' advice is all defensive/fearful actions
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction between 'relax' and paranoid instructions

jokes.text: Three kings put the hammer down / Cause we got a Christmas convoy, ain't she a beautiful thing? / 10-4, King of the Jews
jokes.setup: Trucker song version of Christmas story
jokes.punchline: Mixing CB radio slang with biblical nativity
jokes.explanation: Creative parody mixing trucker culture with Christianity - 'King of the Jews' as CB handle is inspired

jokes.text: Why do things I love always burn?
jokes.setup: Looking at dry Christmas tree
jokes.punchline: Homer's existential question about his relationship with fire and beloved objects
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer introspection - acknowledges his destructive pattern with touching sadness and comedy

jokes.text: Woo-hoo! Maggie's talking!
jokes.setup: Astrolabe speaking, family investigating the voice
jokes.punchline: Homer's desperate attempt to cover by claiming baby is talking
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection attempt showing Homer's poor lying skills

jokes.text: There's a trickledown theory here. If I'm happy, I'm less abusive to the rest of you.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his selfish purchase
jokes.punchline: Framing abuse reduction as economic theory/family benefit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer rationalization - using economic terminology to justify selfishness and casually admitting to being abusive

jokes.text: I am not returnable.
jokes.setup: Family arguing about Homer's wasteful purchase
jokes.punchline: Astrolabe chiming in with perfect timing about its non-returnable status
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing gag - the astrolabe making the situation worse at exactly the wrong moment

jokes.text: This is sadder than Tuesdays with Morrie.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on their sad Christmas situation
jokes.punchline: Mishearing 'Tuesdays with Morrie' but making it somehow sadder
jokes.explanation: Clever mishearing that actually makes the reference sadder - good Marge malapropism

jokes.text: You have to get your hair done at Supercuts. I guess regular cuts just isn't good enough for you.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to deflect criticism about being selfish
jokes.punchline: Criticizing Marge for going to budget hair salon instead of even cheaper option
jokes.explanation: Good Homer deflection showing his warped perspective on what constitutes extravagance

jokes.text: That's not true! I cared what you thought once you found out!
jokes.setup: Marge saying Homer only cares about himself
jokes.punchline: Homer's defense actually proves her point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic failure - his attempt at defense completely undermines itself

jokes.text: I'm gonna parcel my anger out over the next few days and weeks, jabbing at you just when you seem the most content.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if Marge can just yell at him now
jokes.punchline: Marge's methodical plan for psychological warfare
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of Marge's character - showing her capacity for strategic emotional manipulation with clinical precision

jokes.text: Colombia's main export is coffee. Exactly.
jokes.setup: Homer talking to his astrolabe for comfort
jokes.punchline: Random fact somehow validates Homer's emotional state
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor with Homer finding meaning in random trivia

jokes.text: I can't find my way back to the home. I heard you the first five times!
jokes.setup: Blind old man asking for directions
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's been asking the same person repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy setup showing the blind character's confusion

jokes.text: Sir, I'm over here. Woo-hoo. Sorry, pardon me, ma'am. I see you're expecting. May I listen to the baby's heartbeat?
jokes.setup: Blind McGrew trying to interact with Cratchit
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong about location and mistaking man for pregnant woman
jokes.explanation: Good escalating misunderstanding with cringe elements

jokes.text: Oh, McGrew. Once again you've mistaken something for something.
jokes.setup: McGrew making another mistake
jokes.punchline: Generic description of his constant confusion pattern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on the character's running gag with perfect economy

jokes.text: Oh, my God! It's like looking at a cartoon version of myself!
jokes.setup: Homer watching selfish character on TV
jokes.punchline: Not realizing he IS a cartoon character
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-humor - Homer has no awareness he's already a cartoon, making his observation brilliantly ironic

jokes.text: Come on, Death. Leave McGrew alone. Take Tiny Tim!
jokes.setup: Homer getting invested in the Christmas story
jokes.punchline: Rooting for the death of the sympathetic child character
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer humor - completely missing the moral point and siding with death against innocent child

jokes.text: 'Unloved by Al?' No! 'Unloved by all?' No!
jokes.setup: Homer reading his gravestone in nightmare
jokes.punchline: Misreading 'all' as someone named 'Al' and being more upset about the universal rejection
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Homer's reading comprehension issues even in his own nightmares

jokes.text: Yeah. TV writers have been milking that goat for years.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining A Christmas Carol's literary significance
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude assessment of adaptation culture
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-commentary on TV adaptations with typical Homer crudeness

jokes.text: Reform, Ebenezer Urkel! You have alienated everyone who loved you! Did I do that?
jokes.setup: Another Christmas Carol parody
jokes.punchline: Steve Urkel as Scrooge with his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Simple mashup parody - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: It's no use, Captain. He's showin' visions of me future. God, I'm so fat! I said fire!
jokes.setup: Star Trek Christmas Carol parody
jokes.punchline: Scotty more concerned about his weight than the supernatural threat
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment in parody context

jokes.text: Yes. But this time I'm sober-ish.
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out Homer's made promises before
jokes.punchline: Homer's qualifier suggesting he's still somewhat drunk
jokes.explanation: Good Homer qualifier - the 'ish' suffix perfectly captures his current state

jokes.text: These pants smell worse than my old pants. You're welcome.
jokes.setup: Homer giving clothes to hoboes
jokes.punchline: Recipient complaining and Homer's oblivious response
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of charitable giving with Homer's obliviousness to the complaint

jokes.text: A photo cube with pictures of us! And I filed down all the sharp corners. See? Your eye is completely safe! Oh, wow, it just stings a little.
jokes.setup: Homer giving Lenny a thoughtful replacement gift
jokes.punchline: His safety precaution failing immediately
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Homer's well-intentioned incompetence

jokes.text: Oh, your thoughtfulness tastes so good. And tears are the sweetest sauce.
jokes.setup: Marge touched by Homer's gesture with the pork chop
jokes.punchline: Her overly emotional and slightly disturbing description
jokes.explanation: Good Marge moment showing her tendency toward overly poetic emotional expression

jokes.text: All right, now you're starting to creep me out.
jokes.setup: Marge getting overly emotional about his kindness
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest assessment of his wife's intensity
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - Homer recognizing when Marge crosses into uncomfortable territory

jokes.text: I'm just buying that stairway to heaven Jesus sang of. That was Led Zeppelin. Get back to your bong, hippie.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining his charitable motivations
jokes.punchline: Reverend's harsh correction and dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expected religious support with unnecessarily harsh response

jokes.text: I'm jealous of girls because they get to wear dresses. One problem at a time, boy.
jokes.setup: Ned asking if Todd is jealous of Homer
jokes.punchline: Todd revealing much deeper identity issues, Ned's overwhelmed response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - what seemed like simple jealousy reveals complex gender identity issues, Ned's practical response is perfect

jokes.text: Being unselfish is a natural high like hiking or paint thinner.
jokes.setup: Homer describing the joy of being charitable
jokes.punchline: Comparing positive activity to huffing paint
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - equating wholesome activity with substance abuse in casual comparison

jokes.text: And here's another act of Christian charity I pulled out of my butt.
jokes.setup: Homer announcing his skating rink project
jokes.punchline: Crude expression mixed with religious terminology
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer mixing of high and low register language

jokes.text: Here's your skates. Oh, you'll have to take off those boots. Those are my feet. Eew!
jokes.setup: Homer helping someone with skates
jokes.punchline: Discovering the person's feet are in terrible condition
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out humor with physical comedy

jokes.text: Santa Claus is dead. Or he might as well be because there's an even fatter man who's holding families at nice-point.
jokes.setup: Breaking news about Christmas crisis
jokes.punchline: 'Nice-point' as play on gunpoint for Homer's excessive kindness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - 'nice-point' perfectly captures how overwhelming kindness can become threatening

jokes.text: That Homer just burns my waffles. Pain is the cleanser! Pain is the cleanser!
jokes.setup: Ned getting frustrated with Homer's success
jokes.punchline: Mild expression followed by self-flagellation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned escalation - from mild food metaphor to religious extremism instantly

jokes.text: I know that can be a dickens of a doozie!
jokes.setup: Ned offering to fold a map
jokes.punchline: His characteristically mild expression for a simple task
jokes.explanation: Good Ned character moment with his overly polite language

jokes.text: I said 'man,' not 'man-God'! Keep your pants on!
jokes.setup: Ned declaring he'll be the nicest man ever
jokes.punchline: Having to clarify he's not claiming divinity
jokes.explanation: Good Ned moment showing his awareness of religious boundaries while being competitive

jokes.text: What's your angle, pervert?
jokes.setup: Ned giving everyone Christmas presents
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Skinner's suspicious and harsh response to generosity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Agnes Skinner - immediately suspicious and insulting about kindness

jokes.text: You just tweezered my wang!
jokes.setup: Playing Krusty-branded Operation game
jokes.punchline: Krusty's crude version of the game's standard phrases
jokes.explanation: Good character integration - Krusty would absolutely make a crude version of children's game

jokes.text: People would be a lot happier without presents.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining Buddhist philosophy about materialism
jokes.punchline: Homer taking this as actionable advice rather than philosophy
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Homer's misunderstanding - Lisa's wisdom becomes Homer's justification

jokes.text: I'm not going to jail again.
jokes.setup: Police sirens while Homer plans theft
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to previous incarceration
jokes.explanation: Good character detail suggesting Homer's criminal history

jokes.text: You're a hero, Homer J / You're as crafty as a skunk / They'll thank you in the morning for stealing Flanders' junk
jokes.setup: Homer singing while committing theft
jokes.punchline: Self-congratulatory song about criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Good Homer self-delusion - reframing theft as heroism through song

jokes.text: Man, so this is how it feels. I'd better see my shrink and rob his ass!
jokes.setup: Person discovering they've been robbed
jokes.punchline: Deciding to rob their therapist in response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation logic - experiencing crime leads to committing crime against therapist

jokes.text: Do you think it was Papa? I wouldn't put it past him. He stole my gold tooth the night he left.
jokes.setup: Someone wondering who stole Christmas presents
jokes.punchline: Blaming absent father with specific theft example
jokes.explanation: Good dark family history with specific absurd detail about gold tooth theft

jokes.text: He didn't leave. He went to the store. And when he comes back, I'll wave those Pop Tarts right in your face!
jokes.setup: Sister explaining father's absence
jokes.punchline: Delusional defense with specific snack food triumph
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe moment - pathetic delusion with oddly specific Pop Tarts detail

jokes.text: Your behavior, it's like not sanctioned by any governing body. You're like wild.
jokes.setup: Apu confronting Homer about the theft
jokes.punchline: His characteristically formal language breaking down into simple description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu - formal language deteriorating into simple assessment. 'You're like wild' is brilliantly awkward.

jokes.text: That's my last flare. Somebody better come soon. Oh, thank goodness. Rescue dogs. (GROWLING)
jokes.setup: Person celebrating rescue dogs arrival
jokes.punchline: Dogs are growling menacingly instead of rescuing
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with dark turn - rescue becomes threat

jokes.text: Let's just say that on this day a million years ago, a dude was born who most of us think was magic. But others don't. And that's cool. But we're probably right.
jokes.setup: Reverend giving Christmas sermon under restrictions
jokes.punchline: Casual, modern description of Jesus with hedged theology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant religious satire - reducing Christian theology to casual modern language while maintaining faith

jokes.text: Oh, joy! Let's see what we got here. It's a new brassiere! But I don't even have them, but okay. I'll make pretend.
jokes.setup: Man opening Christmas present
jokes.punchline: Getting women's undergarment but accepting it cheerfully
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe humor with character's positive attitude despite inappropriate gift

jokes.text: I ain't got all day, Drama Queen. Get it over with. I will! And then you'll all wish you was nicer to me! Who am I kidding? I ain't gonna jump.
jokes.setup: Moe's annual suicide attempt
jokes.punchline: Crowd rushing him and Moe's immediate capitulation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - his attention-seeking behavior meets reality of audience impatience, leading to immediate backing down

jokes.text: Today is the birthday of our Lord, Jesus Christ, and singer Barbara Mandrell.
jokes.setup: Christmas day astrolabe announcement
jokes.punchline: Equating Jesus Christ with country singer in same fact
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition humor - treating the birth of Christ and Barbara Mandrell as equal facts worthy of announcement
